admin.analytics.cards.totalViewsTooltip: Total number of site page views in the selected period, including anonymous and logged-in users.
admin.analytics.cards.completionsTooltip: Number of tours fully completed (all mandatory steps validated) in the selected period.
admin.analytics.cards.abandonmentsToolti: Number of tours started but not completed. Avg progress shows how far users got before leaving.
admin.analytics.cards.newRegistrationsTo: Number of new user accounts created during the selected period.
admin.analytics.cards.totalUsersTooltip: Total number of registered user accounts on the platform (all time).
admin.analytics.help.title: How to read this dashboard
admin.analytics.help.body: Use the date range presets (7d / 30d / 90d / All time) to filter all metrics. Cards show aggregated totals for the period. The chart shows daily traffic trends. Click 'Steps' on any tour to drill down into per-step analytics. Drop rate indicates what percentage of users who viewed a step did not complete it — lower is better.
admin.analytics.chart.title: Traffic over time
admin.analytics.tourTable.title: Tour performance
admin.analytics.tourTable.colRateTooltip: Completion rate: percentage of users who started the tour and completed all mandatory steps.
admin.analytics.tourTable.colAvgTimeTool: Average time spent per step across all steps of this tour.
admin.analytics.tourTable.colAbandonedTo: Number of times users started this tour but left before finishing.
admin.analytics.tourTable.colSharedToolt: Number of times this tour was shared via the share button.
admin.analytics.stepTable.title: Step analytics
admin.analytics.stepTable.colViewsToolti: Number of users who tapped 'I'm here' and opened this step.
admin.analytics.stepTable.colDoneTooltip: Number of users who successfully validated this step (QR scan or challenge).
admin.analytics.stepTable.colAvgTimeTool: Average time from opening to completing (or leaving) this step.
admin.analytics.stepTable.colDropTooltip: Percentage of users who viewed this step but did not complete it. Calculated relative to the previous step's completions to show funnel loss.
admin.analytics.drilldown.avgDropTooltip: Average percentage of tour progress reached when users abandon. Higher means users get further before dropping off.
admin.analytics.business.title: Business Analytics
admin.analytics.business.premiumBody: Per-business metrics (step views, link clicks, CTR, average time on step) are available to businesses with an active premium subscription.
admin.analytics.business.colStepViewsToo: Number of times users opened a step where this business appears.
admin.analytics.business.colCTRTooltip: Click-through rate: percentage of step views that resulted in any link click (web, phone, or maps).
admin.analytics.business.colAvgTimeToolt: Average time users spent on steps featuring this business.

legal.privacy.intro: StepUp Tours ("we", "us", or "our") is committed to protecting your personal data and your right to privacy. This Privacy Policy describes the information we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and the rights you have under applicable data protection law, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for users in the European Economic Area and the United Kingdom.
legal.privacy.s1_body: StepUp Tours acts as the data controller for the personal data you provide or that we collect when you use our platform. For any data protection enquiries, please use our contact form.
legal.privacy.s2_body:
We collect personal data in the following categories:

### Account Information
• Username and email address (required for registration)
• Password (stored as a one-way cryptographic hash; never in plain text)
• Display name and optional profile details you choose to provide
• Preferred language and country (for personalisation purposes)

### Tour Activity Data
• Tours you start, complete, or mark as a favourite
• Steps completed, XP points earned, and ranking position
• Ratings and written reviews you submit for tours

### Payment Information
• Subscription plan details (plan type, billing cycle, renewal date)
• Donation amounts and timestamps
• We do not store card numbers, bank account details, or any other raw payment credentials. All payment data is processed exclusively by Stripe, Inc., governed by their Privacy Policy.

### Usage and Technical Data
• Device type, operating system, and browser version
• IP address (used for security, fraud prevention, and access logging)
• Pages and features accessed within the platform
• Error logs and performance data used for service improvement
legal.privacy.s3_title: 3. Legal Basis and Purposes of Processing
legal.privacy.s3_body:
We process your personal data only when we have a valid legal basis to do so:

• Performance of a contract: To create and manage your account, process payments, and deliver the services you have requested.
• Legitimate interests: To improve platform quality, detect and prevent fraud, ensure account security, and provide technical support.
• Legal obligation: To comply with applicable laws including tax, accounting, and anti-fraud regulations.
• Consent: To send optional communications such as newsletters where you have explicitly opted in. You may withdraw consent at any time.
legal.privacy.s4_body:
We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. We may share data with trusted third parties strictly as necessary:

• Stripe, Inc.: Payment processing and fraud detection, governed by Stripe's Privacy Policy and GDPR Data Processing Addendum.
• Hosting and infrastructure providers: To operate our servers and databases, all bound by data processing agreements ensuring GDPR-equivalent safeguards.
• Legal authorities: Where required by law, court order, or to protect the rights and safety of our users.

Public profile data — including your username, XP total, and global ranking position — is visible to other platform users by design.
legal.privacy.s5_body: Some of our service providers are located outside the European Economic Area. When we transfer personal data to countries without an adequacy decision by the European Commission, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), or rely on the provider's certification under an equivalent recognised framework.
legal.privacy.s6_body:
Under applicable data protection law (including the GDPR for users in the EEA and UK), you have the following rights:

• Right of access: Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
• Right to rectification: Ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete personal data.
• Right to erasure: Request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten"), subject to legal retention obligations.
• Right to restriction: Ask us to restrict processing in certain circumstances.
• Right to data portability: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
• Right to object: Object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing.
• Right to withdraw consent: Withdraw consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.

To exercise any of these rights, please use our contact form. We will respond within 30 calendar days. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection supervisory authority.
legal.privacy.s7_body: We retain personal data for as long as your account remains active or as needed to deliver our services. If you delete your account, we will delete or anonymise your personal data within 30 calendar days, except where required by law to retain it longer (for example, financial records may be retained for up to 7 years).
legal.privacy.s8_body:
We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data:

• Encrypted connections (HTTPS/TLS 1.2+) for all data in transit
• Passwords stored using industry-standard adaptive hashing (bcrypt)
• Role-based access controls limiting data access to authorised personnel only
• Regular security reviews, dependency audits, and vulnerability assessments
• Anomalous login detection and account protection mechanisms

No method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure. We encourage you to use a strong, unique password and report any suspected security incident via our contact form.
legal.privacy.s9_body: StepUp Tours is not directed at children under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have inadvertently collected data from a child, please contact us immediately via our contact form and we will take prompt steps to delete that information.
legal.privacy.s10_body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make material changes, we will update this page and, where required by law, notify you directly. Continued use of the platform after changes are posted constitutes your acknowledgement of the revised policy.
legal.cookie.intro: This Cookie Policy explains what cookies are, which ones StepUp Tours uses, and how you can control them. It should be read alongside our Privacy Policy. By continuing to use our platform, you acknowledge this policy.
legal.cookie.s1_body:
Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website or use a web application. They allow the platform to remember information about your visit — such as your login session or language preference — making your experience more efficient and personalised.

Cookies may be first-party (set by us) or third-party (set by services we use). They may be session cookies (deleted when you close your browser) or persistent cookies (retained for a defined period).
legal.cookie.s2_body:
StepUp Tours uses cookies and similar technologies for the following purposes:

• Authentication: To keep you signed in across pages and sessions.
• Preferences: To remember your language and interface settings.
• Tour progress: To track completed steps so you can resume where you left off.
• Platform analytics: To understand how users interact with features and improve performance.
• Security and fraud prevention: To detect anomalous access patterns and protect user accounts.
• Payment processing: To manage secure checkout sessions when you subscribe or donate.
legal.cookie.s3_body:
### Strictly Necessary
Essential for the platform to function. Without these cookies, services such as authentication and tour progress tracking cannot be provided. They do not require consent and cannot be disabled without impairing the platform.

### Functional
Allow us to remember choices you make — such as your preferred language — and provide a personalised experience. Disabling these may degrade your experience.

### Analytics
Used to collect aggregated, anonymised data about platform usage to help us prioritise improvements. We do not sell analytics data to third parties.

### Payment (Third-party)
When you initiate a subscription or donation, Stripe may set cookies to manage the payment session and detect fraud. Governed exclusively by Stripe's Privacy Policy.
legal.cookie.s4_body:
You have several options to control cookies:

• Browser settings: Most browsers let you view, block, or delete cookies via their settings menu. Disabling certain cookies may prevent key features from working.
• In-app consent: Withdraw consent by clearing browser local storage or app data. The consent prompt will reappear on your next visit.
• Third-party opt-out tools: For analytics cookies set by third parties, use the opt-out mechanisms provided by those services.

Disabling strictly necessary cookies will impair core platform functionality, including the ability to stay logged in.
legal.cookie.s5_body: Some cookies may process personal data (for example, a session identifier linked to your account). Where this is the case, processing is covered by our Privacy Policy, which sets out the legal basis, purposes, and your rights. We encourage you to read our Privacy Policy alongside this Cookie Policy.
legal.cookie.s6_body: We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect changes in technology, legislation, or our operational practices. When we make material changes, we will update this page and, where appropriate, notify you through the platform.
legal.terms.intro: These Terms of Use ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the StepUp Tours platform, including our mobile application and website (collectively, the "Service"). By creating an account or using the Service in any way, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.
legal.terms.s1_body: StepUp Tours is a gamified self-guided tour platform that enables users to discover cities and cultural landmarks by following curated routes. Users earn experience points (XP) by completing steps along a route, climbing a global ranking and unlocking achievements. Professional guides (Tour Creators) may publish and monetise tours through subscription plans and voluntary user donations.
legal.terms.s5_body:
### Subscription Plans
Tour Creators may subscribe to a paid plan to unlock features such as publishing multiple tours and featuring businesses at tour stops. Fees and features are as displayed in the application.

### Billing and Renewal
Subscriptions are billed on a recurring basis (monthly or annually) unless cancelled before the renewal date. You may cancel auto-renewal at any time through your dashboard; cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period with no refund for the remaining period.

### Payment Processing
All payments are processed by Stripe, Inc. We do not store your card details. By making a payment, you also agree to Stripe's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

### Donations
Users may voluntarily donate to tour guides upon completing a tour. Donations are non-refundable and distributed according to the platform's configured revenue split.

### Taxes
All listed prices are exclusive of applicable taxes unless stated otherwise. You are responsible for any taxes applicable to your purchase in your jurisdiction.
legal.terms.s7_body:
All content provided by StepUp Tours — including the application software, design, graphics, logos, text, and gamification mechanics — is the exclusive property of StepUp Tours or its licensors, protected by applicable intellectual property laws. Nothing in these Terms grants you any right other than the limited licence to use the Service as set out herein.

You may not reproduce, distribute, modify, create derivative works from, or commercially exploit any StepUp Tours content without our prior written consent.
legal.terms.s8_body: The Service may integrate with or link to third-party services (e.g., mapping providers, Stripe for payments, Google for social sign-in). These services are governed by their own terms and privacy policies. We are not responsible for the content, practices, or policies of any third-party service, and your use of such services is at your own risk.
legal.terms.s9_body:
The Service is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis, without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

We do not warrant that the Service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or that information within the Service (including tour content) is accurate, complete, or current. Tour content is provided for informational and entertainment purposes. You are responsible for your own safety while following a tour route.
legal.terms.s11_body:
You may delete your account at any time through your profile settings or via our contact form. Upon deletion, your personal data will be handled in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

We reserve the right to suspend or permanently terminate your access to the Service at our sole discretion, without notice or liability, if we believe you have violated these Terms or engaged in fraudulent activity.
legal.terms.s12_body: We may update these Terms from time to time. When we make material changes, we will update this page and, where appropriate, notify you through the platform or by email. Your continued use of the Service after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
legal.terms.s13_body:
These Terms are governed by the laws of the European Union and applicable national law. Any unresolved dispute shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts.

If you are a consumer in the European Union, you may also use the EU Online Dispute Resolution platform at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/ to resolve disputes out of court.

Blank line = new paragraph

faq.intro: Find answers to the most common questions about StepUp Tours — self-guided city tours with audio narration, step-by-step navigation, and a gamified experience you can enjoy at your own pace.
faq.a1: StepUp Tours is a self-guided city tour app that lets you explore cities at your own pace. Each tour is made up of a series of stops (steps) spread across the city. When you arrive at each stop you unlock a rich description read aloud by our AI audio guide, a Street View of the location, and nearby points of interest. Complete all mandatory stops to finish the tour and earn XP points.
faq.q2: Do I need an account to use the app?
faq.a2: No account is required to browse and view tours. However, you need a free account to complete steps, earn XP, save favourites, rate tours, and support guides with a donation. Sign up takes less than a minute.
faq.a3: The app is available in English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, and Greek. Tour content and the audio guide are available in all supported languages — except Greek, where the audio guide is not yet available. You can switch language at any time from your profile.
faq.q4: How do I start a tour?
faq.a4: Browse the tour catalogue on the home screen, tap a tour to see its details, then tap "Start Tour". There is no fixed start time, no booking, and no guide to wait for — you go at your own pace whenever you like.
faq.q5: Can I complete steps in any order?
faq.a5: Yes — steps can be completed in any order you choose. Feel free to skip around, double back, or take detours. Only mandatory steps count toward tour completion; optional steps award bonus XP but do not block the finish.
faq.q6: How do I get to a step location?
faq.a6: Tap "How to get there" on any step card. Choose your preferred transport mode — Walking, Cycling, Public Transport, or Driving. The app will open Google Maps (or Apple Maps on iOS) with turn-by-turn directions to the exact location. Once you arrive, switch back to StepUp Tours to confirm your presence and unlock the step.
faq.q7: How do I complete a step?
faq.a7: When you are physically at the step location, the app detects your position and shows a confirmation prompt: "Are you at [location name]?". Tap "Yes, I'm here" to unlock the step content — description, audio guide, and Street View. When you are ready, tap the green "Mark as Completed" button to record the step.
faq.q8: What happens when I complete all steps?
faq.a8: A congratulations screen appears showing the XP you have earned. You are invited to rate the tour from 1 to 5 stars, and you can optionally leave a donation to support the guide who created it. Both the rating and the donation are entirely optional.
faq.a9: Each step includes an AI-generated audio narration of the location description. Tap the play button in the step card to listen while you explore. You can pause, stop, and resume at any time. The audio guide is available in all supported languages except Greek.
faq.q10: Can I adjust the playback speed?
faq.a10: Yes. The audio player offers five speed options: 0.75×, 1× (normal), 1.25×, 1.5×, and 2×. Tap the speed button in the player to cycle through them.
faq.q11: How do I earn XP points?
faq.a11: You earn 20 XP the first time you complete a tour. Optional steps may award bonus XP. Points accumulate in your profile over time.
faq.a12: The global ranking leaderboard lists all users ordered by total XP earned. Your position updates automatically after every tour completion. Tap "Ranking" in the navigation menu to see where you stand.
faq.a13: Logged-in users can bookmark any tour by tapping the heart icon on the tour card or detail page. Favourites are saved to your account and accessible from the Favourites tab so you can quickly find tours you want to do later.
faq.a14: No — donations are completely voluntary. After completing a tour you will see an optional donation prompt to support the guide who created it. You can close this prompt at any time with no penalty. We will never charge you without your explicit consent.
faq.a15: Your donation goes directly to the tour creator (the guide). A small platform fee is deducted to cover payment processing and platform maintenance. The exact revenue split is shown transparently on screen before you confirm any payment.
faq.q16: What is the minimum donation amount?
faq.a16: The minimum donation is €1. There is no maximum — you are free to contribute as much as you like.
faq.q17: Do I need an internet connection?
faq.a17: Yes. An active internet connection is required to load tour content, stream the audio guide, show Street View, and save your progress to your account. We recommend using Wi-Fi or a mobile data connection throughout the tour.
faq.q18: The audio guide is not available — what happened?
faq.a18: The audio guide is currently not available in Greek. If you are using the app in Greek, you will see a notice on the step card. Switch to any other supported language (English, Spanish, French, German, or Italian) to enable the audio guide.
